---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
---

<!-- the section below is shown only if rendering is beamer from latex. (and format:revealjs is commmented) -->

::: {.content-visible when-format="beamer"}
```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright \emph{1.1 - Tests and Measurement}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```
:::

## A fragment of a test
```{=latex}
\includegraphics[width=0.9\textwidth]{Figures/Becks_1.png}
```

## A fragment of test scoring
```{=latex}
\includegraphics[width=0.9\textwidth]{Figures/Becks_2.png}
```

## Definition of Test

```{=latex}
\setlength{\fboxrule}{1.5pt}

"A test is a
\alt<2->{\fcolorbox{red}{white}{\rule{0pt}{12pt}\hspace{2pt}systematic procedure\hspace{2pt}}}{systematic procedure}
for\\
\alt<3->{\fcolorbox{red}{white}{\rule{0pt}{12pt}\hspace{2pt}observing a person's behavior\hspace{2pt}}}{observing a person's behavior}
and for its
\alt<4->{\fcolorbox{red}{white}{\rule{0pt}{12pt}\hspace{2pt}description with the help of a numerical scale\hspace{2pt}}}{description with the help of a numerical scale}
or a category system.

\begin{flushright}
  \emph{(Cronbach, 1990, p.32)}
\end{flushright}
\pause
```

## Constructs
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[scale=0.05]{Figures/triangle.png}
\end{center}

with psychological tests the interest is never in the behavior itself but in the \textbf{construct} measured by that behavior.

\textbf{Construct}: an unobservable psychological concept 

## Type of tests

- **maximal performance** \pause
- **typical behaviour** \pause

## A definition of Measurement
\pause

_"We may say that measurement, in the broadest sense, is defined as the assignment of numerals to objects or events, according to rules."_

*Stevens (1946)*
\vfill
\pause
Depending on the rule, different operations can be made with the numbers obtained with the measurement.

## Our previous example
```{=latex}
\includegraphics[width=0.9\textwidth]{Figures/Becks_2.png}
```

<!--
## Measurement Scales According to Stevens

:::{.small}
\scriptsize
For Stevens, what is important is to define the measurement scale, which relates objects to numbers.

\tiny
| Scale        | Description                                                                 | Example                         | Possible Operations                                   |
|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| **Nominal** | Classification into categories without implicit order                         | Sex (M/F), Colors, Nationality  | Counting, mode, checking equality/difference      |
| **Ordinal** | Classification with order, but distances between categories have no meaning   | Position in a ranking, Educational level | Ordering, medians, percentiles                     |
| **Interval** | Measurement with order and equal intervals, but without meaningful absolute zero | Temperature in °C, IQ           | Addition, Subtraction, Mean, Standard deviation, z-score                |
| **Ratio** | Measurement with order, equal intervals and zero has meaning          |  Age, Years of education, reaction times | All arithmetic operations (×, ÷, +, −), z-score, ratios|

**Legend**  
- *Scale*: type of measurement according to Stevens' classification.  
- *Description*: main characteristics of the scale.  
- *Example*: real variables that fall into that scale.  
- *Possible operations*: mathematical and statistical transformations allowed by the data.
:::
-->

## A new questionnaire

Imagine having this questionnaire:
\scriptsize

1. Do you feel depressed? (No = 0, Yes = 1) \pause
2. Do you sometimes feel like you're having a bad day? (No = 0, Yes = 1) \pause
3. Do you sometimes feel that there is no hope in your life? (No = 0, Yes = 1) \pause
4. Do you enjoy romantic movies? (No = 1, Yes = 0) \pause

\vfill
\normalsize
The final score is obtained by summing the scores (0/1) of each question and a score > 3 is a very good indicator of Depression. 
\pause

\textbf{Would you consider this a "test" and a "measurement?"}
